what_it_does: Documents the `company` command-line control surface for operating the running system: commands for services, local models, GPU budgeting, config/combos, and the file layout.
observations: `company` is a single CLI that drives systemd using the self-describing registry `../services.json`.
Nothing auto-starts at boot except `ollama` and `tailscaled`; the machine boots cold and services are brought up manually.
Targets can be a service key, a group (`core`, `brain`, `voice`, `models`, `reach`), `all`, or a combo prefixed with `@`.
The resource manager treats the 16 GB GPU as a budget: it refuses starts that would exceed free VRAM, supports `--evict` largest-first, `--force` override, and `--wait` for real load telemetry.
`company ensure MODEL|SERVICE` is described as the ONE gated launch/select actuator shared by the cognition engine and the CLI.
Model serve settings live in `services.json` and run through one launcher (`../serve_model.sh`); tuning is done from the CLI.
Combos are named service sets; loadout variants can `extends` a base combo and override with `swap`, `add`, and `remove`.
Status uses symbols: ▶ running · ◐ active-no-port-yet · ✖ failed · · stopped · and marks hand-started services as `RUNNING (unmanaged)`.
The file maps implementation files: `app.py`, `registry.py`, `systemd.py`, `gpu.py`, `telemetry.py`, `models.py`, `bench.py`, `render.py`, plus `services.json`, `AGENTS.md`, and `UPDATING.md`.
standouts: Resource manager refuses over-budget starts and can auto-evict running GPU services largest-first.
`company ensure` is the single shared actuator for on-demand model residency used by both the engine and the CLI.
Cold-boot policy: nothing auto-starts, operators explicitly bring up only what a task needs.
conventions: One command surface for all operational tasks.
Single launcher script `../serve_model.sh`; no per-model shell scripts.
Fail-loud behavior for resource conflicts and missing prerequisites.
Running detection uses `systemctl is-active`, not ports, because model services share ports.
Telemetry feeds the budget from measured VRAM and load times rather than estimates.
concerns: `--force` lets the operator override the VRAM refusal, which could destabilize the GPU; it is documented but inherently risky.
Model services share ports, so port-based health/running detection is explicitly unreliable.
No mention of rollback or dry-run when changing configs or swapping models.
Combo variant resolution rules are described at a high level; the exact JSON schema and cycle/error behavior are not shown here.
notes: This README is the user-facing contract for the `company` CLI, covering commands, GPU scheduling, model configuration, combos, and the implementation file map.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema of `../services.json` for services, groups, and combos?
How does `gpu.py` measure VRAM, and what metric does largest-first eviction use (estimate, `gpu_util`, measured VRAM)?
What is the Session Fabric / session-supervisor service and how does it spawn the Claude Code fleet?
What are the precise service memberships of each group (`core`, `brain`, `voice`, `models`, `reach`)?
How are combo variants resolved in JSON (fields, inheritance order, cycle detection)?
What is the relationship between the top-level `company` command and the `../company` launcher?
What is the API shape of `capabilities.ensure_resident`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as the use guide for the `company` control surface, and the file matches that by documenting commands, resource management, configuration, combos, and file layout.
apparent_themes: Explicit manual lifecycle and cold boot
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish `company` as the single, predictable, human-and-agent-operable control plane for a local GPU-backed system, with clear resource budgeting and reusable loadout patterns.
novelty: The combination of a single CLI that doubles as the shared engine actuator (`ensure`), largest-first auto-eviction against a measured VRAM budget, and combo loadout variants (`extends`/`swap`) is fairly distinctive.
contribution: It contributes the user-facing contract and conceptual map for the `company` CLI: command vocabulary, resource-manager semantics, config/combo/loadout patterns, and pointers to the implementation files and registry.
summary_for_embedding: README documenting the `company` CLI, a unified control surface for systemd-backed services, local models, and GPU VRAM budgeting, including commands, resource-manager behavior, model configuration, combos, and file layout.
intention_for_embedding: To teach operators and agents how to use the `company` command to start/stop services, manage GPU-resident models, tune configs, and run loadouts.
